magic_system.gap: The climactic revelation in Ch 19 — that Bãi Chờ is a 'waiting room' and dissolution equals liberation/reincarnation — is not established in the hard rules section. The climax depends on a late-introduced rule that a reader cannot anticipate from previously established mechanics. Additionally, the criterion for 'true death' versus reincarnation ('bám víu vào bản sắc quá chặt') is stated but not defined with enough specificity for a writer to adjudicate edge cases.
magic_system.fix: Add one hard rule to world.md that implies the waiting-room nature of the island without revealing it outright — e.g., 'Người tan biến không để lại linh hồn. Chỉ còn quần áo và đất bằng phẳng. Như họ đi đâu đó, không phải hết.' Seed the reincarnation possibility through soft phenomena (hoa dặm's rootlessness, the well showing living relatives who resemble the dead). Define 'bám víu' with 2-3 concrete examples of what causes true death versus reincarnation.
magic_system.note: The one-way communication rule, memory-as-existence mechanics, and Lịch's life-force writing cost are all excellent. Societal implications are explored with rare specificity — the feudal economy, the 'làm quên' crime, family power dynamics distorted by who on the mainland remembers whom. The system is testable across courtroom, negotiation, and confrontation scenarios. Close to an 8 but the late-stage rule introduction for the climax is a meaningful gap.

world_history.gap: CRITICAL: Cụ Ba's timeline is internally contradictory across all documents. He is described as dying in 1954 (characters.md, canon.md), yet he has 'ba trăm năm trên đảo,' has 'thấy hàng trăm người tan biến trong ba trăm năm,' and his secret #2 describes an event 'ba trăm năm trước' when he allowed someone to contact the mainland. If he died in 1954 and the current year is ~2015, he has been on the island approximately 61 years. This contradiction affects his characterization, his authority, and the backstory justification for the island's isolation policy.
world_history.fix: Two options: (A) Change Cụ Ba's death year to ~1720-1750 (early island period), making him a genuine founding-era figure. This strengthens his authority — he truly has 300 years of experience. His wife's survival and four-generation remembrance still work if his family lineage is established as old Nghệ An fishing stock. (B) Change all '300 years' references to 'sáu mươi năm' — but this weakens the character significantly and makes the 17-person mass dissolution event happen in the 1950s-60s, which changes the political context. Option A is strongly recommended.
world_history.note: History-to-plot connections are otherwise strong. The 1884-1945 colonial period reducing remembrance, the 1954-1975 war creating both new arrivals and new forgetting, the post-1990 safety era starving the island of new blood — each maps to current faction tensions. The Nghệ An origin is culturally specific and justified. But the Cụ Ba contradiction is too large to ignore.

geography_and_culture.gap: Sensory signatures for locations are described but not systematized. A writer setting a scene at Bãi Đón versus Làng Giữa versus Mũi Chờ would need to invent specific combinations of sound, smell, texture, and quality of light for each. The descriptions exist (phosphorescent sand at Bãi Đón, smoke and fish at Làng Giữa, wind and worn stone at Mũi Chờ) but could be consolidated into a quick-reference sensory palette for each location.
geography_and_culture.fix: Add a 5-row table to world.md — one row per major location, columns for: primary smell, secondary smell, dominant sound, quality of light (morning/afternoon/night), ground texture, emotional register. This gives a writer instant environmental anchors without invention.
geography_and_culture.note: Cultural specificity is a genuine strength. The thắp hương custom, thuốc lào smoking, canh chua with tự-mặn well water, áo bà ba clothing, 'làm quên' as the ultimate crime, the Saturday silent meal — these are authentically Vietnamese and generate real conflict. The feudal economy emerging from the magic system is elegant. The taboo on naming the vanished is culturally resonant and psychologically plausible. Score reflects that a writer could draft location-based scenes with minimal invention beyond sensory systematization.

lore_interconnection.gap: The external threat subplot (Meridian oil company, military interest, Liên's journalist contact) is modular rather than organically emergent. If you remove the magic system, the internal politics and class structure collapse — excellent. But if you remove the Meridian subplot, the internal story still works. The external threat creates time pressure but doesn't emerge from the world's internal logic. It could be replaced with any ticking clock without changing the magic, politics, or character dynamics.
lore_interconnection.fix: Tie the external threat more deeply to the magic system. Example: the 'bất thường từ trường' that Meridian detects is caused by the island's magic — meaning the more people remember the dead (Lịch's goal), the more visible the island becomes to instruments. This makes Lịch's solution (publish, make millions remember) directly cause the threat he's trying to prevent. The cure IS the poison. This transforms a modular external threat into an internally generated dilemma.
lore_interconnection.note: Internal interconnection is strong: magic → politics (Hội Đồng = most-remembered), magic → economy (đặc/mờ exchange system), magic → family dynamics (spouse remembered more = more power), magic → geography (island shrinks/grows with life force), magic → culture (Saturday meal, well-gazing, naming taboo). The external threat is the only weakly-connected element.

iceberg_depth.gap: The hoa dặm, nước giếng visions, and đồ vật từ đất liền are presented as unexplained soft magic — which is fine for the novel. But the planning documents should know the answers even if the novel doesn't reveal them. Currently, the docs say 'no one knows' and 'don't explain' without specifying what the author believes is actually happening. Are the flowers a byproduct of the magic? A manifestation of the island's consciousness? A natural phenomenon of a place built from grief? The author's silence here reads as genuine mystery in the novel but as a planning gap in the documents.
iceberg_depth.fix: Add a 'Writer's Private Knowledge' section to world.md that states the author's understanding of each soft magic element — not for inclusion in the novel, but to ensure consistency in how they behave. E.g., 'Hoa dặm: author believes they are the island's dreams, which is why they have no roots (the island doesn't need roots to exist) and why they smell different to each person (they reflect the rememberer, not the remembered).' This ensures a writer handles soft magic consistently without over-explaining it in the text.
iceberg_depth.note: The implied metaphysical system (reincarnation, the distinction between holding on and letting go, the question of whether island residents are 'real people' or 'memory constructs') creates genuine depth. The rule that only biển Đông drownings arrive suggests a larger geography of afterlife options that's never explored — good iceberg. The 'thirst' of newborns who were previously drowned souls is haunting.

character_depth.gap: Cô Năm Thảo and Thầy Tuấn lack full Ghost → Wound → Lie → Want → Need chains. Both appear in the outline (Cô Năm smells oil in Ch 21, Thầy Tuấn is the island's counselor figure) but neither has the psychological depth of the major characters. A writer drafting scenes involving them would need to invent their internal logic.
character_depth.fix: Add abbreviated depth chains for both: Cô Năm (Ghost: husband survived the boat that killed her, now he's dying and with him goes her existence → Lie: 'existing through one person's love is enough' → Need: accept that being remembered by few is not lesser than being remembered by many). Thầy Tuấn (Ghost: died before ordination, arrived on island without the God he was training to serve → Lie: 'faith requires proof, and this place proves nothing' → Need: find that sitting with people in silence IS ministry, with or without God).
character_depth.note: The six major character chains are tight and causally linked. Lịch's lie ('if enough people remember') logically follows from watching his mother slip away — he couldn't hold her, so now he tries to hold everyone. Cụ Ba's lie ('isolation = protection') follows from the trauma of the 17-person dissolution he caused. Phương's arc from passive acceptance to genuine wanting is well-constructed. Chị Tư's baby-that-never-was is a wound that directly feeds her writing compulsion. Liên's survivor's guilt drives every choice. The chains hold up under causal scrutiny.

character_distinctiveness.gap: While the 8-dimension speech analyses are thorough, the actual dialogue examples in the outline chapters sometimes default to a uniform register. When Lịch speaks in Ch 7 ('Tui tìm câu trả lời') or Ch 11 ('Cô mệt rồi. Nghỉ đi.'), the academic complexity and 'Cô thấy không?' tic from his character sheet don't consistently appear. A writer would need to consciously apply the speech patterns rather than having them emerge naturally from the outline.
character_distinctiveness.fix: Add one signature line of dialogue per chapter per speaking character in the outline — not full conversations, but one line that embodies their speech pattern. This forces the outline to demonstrate distinctiveness rather than just describe it. E.g., Ch 7 Lịch should say something like 'Dữ liệu cho thấy... không, thôi, tui nói vậy nghe vô tình quá' (starting academic, catching himself, becoming vulnerable — his pattern).
character_distinctiveness.note: The structural distinctiveness is strong: Cụ Ba's 90% declaratives vs. Phương's 50/50 questions, Lịch's self-interruption vs. Luyện's other-interruption, Chị Tư's mid-sentence pauses vs. Liên's self-answering questions. Metaphor domains don't overlap (Lịch: water/science, Cụ Ba: earth/sea, Phương: cooking/plants, Luyện: sailing/combat). Register differences (Lịch's Bắc 'tôi' vs. islanders' 'tui', Chị Tư's Nam pha Nghệ An) are well-maintained.

character_secrets.gap: Several secrets are emotional revelations rather than plot-altering information. Phương's secret #2 (she loves Lịch) and Chị Tư's secret #2 (she saw her unborn child in the well) are poignant but don't change the plot's trajectory if revealed. Contrast with Liên's secret (she contacted a journalist), which — when revealed — creates the ticking clock for Act III. The secrets need more plot-activation.
character_secrets.fix: Upgrade at least one emotional secret to a plot-secret. Example: Phương's secret could be that she knows her mother isn't just forgetting due to Alzheimer's — her mother found a way to deliberately forget (moved away, threw out photos, stopped speaking Phương's name) because the grief was destroying her. If Phương reveals this, it reframes 'forgetting' from passive tragedy to active self-preservation, directly challenging Lịch's lie that memory is always salvific.
character_secrets.note: The strongest secrets are plot-loaded: Lịch's notebook makes people 'đặc' (revealed → challenges his entire methodology), Cụ Ba caused 17 deaths (revealed → explains and complicates his authoritarianism), Liên contacted journalist (revealed → creates irreversible timeline pressure). Lịch's mother not being on the island is an emotional secret that functions as plot because it removes his personal stake in the island's existence. The mix is good; the gap is that some secrets lack activation potential.

outline_completeness.gap: The external threat (Meridian, the article) lacks a resolution path. Ch 24 notes 'tàu Meridian — xa hơn hôm qua' and 'bài báo sắp ra, không thể ngăn' but these are observations, not resolutions. For an outline that is otherwise meticulously complete, the absence of any closure — even ambiguous closure — for the primary external pressure is a gap. A writer reaching Ch 24 would need to decide: does the article publish? Does the military arrive? Does the island's magic hide it from radar? The outline should commit to at least a narrative stance.
outline_completeness.fix: Add a final beat to Ch 24 or a note to the author specifying the intended stance on the external threat. Recommended: the article publishes, but the island's magic prevents discovery (satellites see only water, ships are deflected by the same force that trapped Luyện). The threat was always Lịch's fear, not an actual danger — the real danger was always internal (holding on vs. letting go). This thematizes the external threat as another form of the lie.
outline_completeness.note: Otherwise excellent. Every chapter has POV, location, Save the Cat beat, emotional arc, try-fail cycle type, specific beats, plants, payoffs, character movement, the lie's status, and word count. The 24-chapter structure hits all Save the Cat milestones at correct percentages. The three quiet chapters (9, 13, 23) are strategically placed. Try-fail distribution is 63%, meeting the 60%+ threshold. This is a draftable outline.

foreshadowing_balance.gap: The climactic revelation in Ch 19 — that Bãi Chờ is a 'phòng chờ' (waiting room), that dissolution equals liberation/reincarnation for most, and that only those who cling too tightly to identity truly die — is not in the foreshadowing ledger. This is the single most important plot twist, and it has no planted seeds. Thread #16 (Chị Tư's unborn child) vaguely points toward the 'đi thẳng' payoff, but the ledger entry is vague ('đi thẳng') rather than specific.
foreshadowing_balance.fix: Add 2-3 threads that foreshadow the waiting-room revelation without spoiling it: (A) Thread: 'Island residents never describe where they were before arriving — only the moment of drowning' (Plant Ch 1, Reinforce Ch 9, Payoff Ch 19: they don't remember an afterlife because there wasn't one — they went straight from water to island). (B) Thread: 'Lịch's inexplicable thirst since birth' (Plant Ch 2, Reinforce Ch 7, Payoff Ch 19: he was nearly-drowned in a past life and reincarnated with the taste of salt in his mouth — the island recognizes him because he's been through this before).
foreshadowing_balance.note: The 20 tracked threads are well-managed with appropriate plant-to-payoff distances. Thread types are varied (symbol, action, dialogue, object, event). Red herrings are identified. The Lịch-m vida-force thread (#2, #3) is particularly well-seeded across 7+ chapters. But the absence of the climactic twist from the ledger is a significant oversight.

internal_consistency.gap: Multiple contradictions and errors across documents, with one critical timeline contradiction:
internal_consistency.fix: See contradictions_found array for specific issues. Priority fix: Resolve Cụ Ba's timeline (change death year to ~1720s or revise all '300 years' to ~60 years). Then clean language artifacts (眼里水, Mathoáng, filled lungs, xdůžne, panicked). Then reconcile the island-growth rule with Lịch's anomalous presence.
internal_consistency.note: The Cụ Ba contradiction alone would cap this at 6. The additional errors (mixed-language artifacts, the island-growth rule tension, the premature birth addition in Ch 2 not reflected in character docs) bring it to 5. These are all fixable but indicate the documents need a reconciliation pass before drafting.

voice_clarity.gap: The voice document contains language artifacts that undermine its authority: Chinese characters '眼里水' mixed into Vietnamese text, garbled text 'Mathoáng qua rồi mất,' English intrusions 'filled lungs' and 'panicked,' and the typo 'xdůžne' (in characters.md). A voice doc that cannot maintain its own linguistic register signals uncertainty about the voice it's prescribing.
voice_clarity.fix: Clean all language artifacts from the voice and character documents. Replace '眼里水' with the Vietnamese equivalent or describe the gesture without the Chinese term. Replace 'Mathoáng qua rồi mất' with 'Thoáng qua rồi mất.' Replace 'filled lungs' with 'phổi ngập nước.' Replace 'panicked' with 'hoảng loạn.' Replace 'xdůžne' with 'chiều' or the intended Vietnamese word. Run a full pass for any remaining English/Chinese/garbled intrusions.
voice_clarity.note: The voice definition itself is excellent: 'U buồn nhưng không bi lụy' with the metaphor of 'khói bếp lò chờ tàn' is precise and actionable. The sentence rhythm rules (long/flowing for daily life, short/fragmented for fading, unfinished for unresolvable pain) are specific. The vocabulary wells (water/salt, smoke/hearth, breath/cycles) give a writer concrete lexical boundaries. The exemplar passage successfully demonstrates the voice. The anti-rules ('Không romance hóa sự chết đuối,' 'Không cho nhân vật phát biểu triết lý dài') are clear. Score reflects the document's own failure to maintain its prescribed register.

canon_coverage.gap: Key plot revelations from the outline are not documented in the canon. Specifically: (1) The Ch 19 revelation that Bãi Chờ is a waiting room and dissolution = reincarnation/liberation is absent. (2) The mechanism by which Lịch's notebook writes with 'sinh khí' (life force) is not in the canon. (3) The criterion for 'true death' versus reincarnation is not documented. (4) Lịch's premature birth and newborn thirst (added in Ch 2 outline) are not in the canon.
canon_coverage.fix: Add a 'Plot Revelations' section to canon.md documenting: (1) Bãi Chờ's true nature as waiting room, (2) The life-force writing mechanism, (3) The clinging/letting-go criterion for post-dissolution fate, (4) Any character backstory additions from the outline that weren't in the original character docs. Cross-reference each to its source chapter.
canon_coverage.note: Coverage is otherwise comprehensive. Geography, timeline, magic rules, events, characters, politics, culture, sensory details, and writer rules are all present and sourced. The internal contradiction flag for Cụ Ba is good practice. The relationship matrix is useful. The gap is that the canon wasn't updated to reflect late additions to the outline.

found: Mixed Chinese characters: '眼里水' in voice doc vocabulary wells section
Garbled text: 'Mathoáng qua rồi mất' in voice doc (likely meant 'Thoáng qua rồi mất')
English intrusion: 'filled lungs' in voice doc body-before-emotion section ('lồng ngực nặng như nước biển filled lungs')
English intrusion: 'panicked' in voice doc anti-rules ('Chết đuối là panicked, là sống sot')
Typo: 'sống sot' should be 'sống sót' in voice doc
Garbled text: 'xdůžne' in Phương's secret #1 in characters.md (likely meant 'chiều' or 'xa xăm')
Mixed Chinese: '要不要驱逐' in world.md (Chinese for 'whether to expel')
Mixed Chinese: '每' used as 'mỗi' in world.md origin legend
English intrusion: 'deliberately' in voice doc sentence rhythm section ('Lặp từ deliberately')
English intrusion: 'of' in world.md flower description ('hoa of dặm đường')
English intrusion: 'Volume' in world.md fading description ('như Volume đang xoay xuống')
Mixed Chinese: '变得僵硬' in world.md body reflection rule
English structure: 'red herring #1' in outline — English critical term in Vietnamese document
Consistent use of English Save the Cat terminology ('Beat', 'Opening Image', 'Catalyst') throughout Vietnamese outline — acceptable as technical terms but inconsistent with document language
slop_in_planning_docs.note: The frequency of mixed-language intrusions suggests the planning documents were generated or edited with tools that left artifacts. The underlying creative content is strong, but the documents need a linguistic cleanup pass. Most critically, the Vietnamese exemplar prose and dialogue are clean and natural — the slop is confined to planning/meta text, not the creative writing samples.

contradictions_found: CRITICAL: Cụ Ba died in 1954 (characters.md) but is described as having 'ba trăm năm trên đảo' and making decisions 'ba trăm năm trước.' If current year is ~2015, he has been on the island ~61 years. This affects his characterization, authority, and the backstory for the island's isolation policy.

MODERATE: World.md states 'đảo không lớn lên khi người mới đến — chỉ giữ nguyên hoặc thu nhỏ' but Ch 12 shows the island growing when Lịch arrived. The outline treats this as anomalous (Lịch is unique), but the hard rule in world.md doesn't account for exceptions.
MINOR: Ch 2 outline adds 'Bệnh viện Đức. Sinh non' about Lịch's birth (premature), which is not in his character doc. This is an addition, not a contradiction, but should be reconciled.

MINOR: World.md states 'Thời gian trên đảo = thời gian thực' but also documents rice growing in 40 days instead of 3 months. Either the accelerated growth is magical (not stated as such) or it contradicts the real-time rule.
ARTIFACT: Multiple language intrusions (Chinese, English, garbled text) in planning documents as detailed in slop findings.
top_3_improvements: FIX CỤ BA TIMELINE (affects world_history, internal_consistency, canon_coverage): Change his death year from 1954 to ~1720-1730, making him a genuine founding-era figure with 300 years of experience. Update all references. This is a single edit that resolves the most critical contradiction and strengthens the character.

ADD FORESHADOWING FOR CLIMACTIC REVELATION (affects foreshadowing_balance, magic_system, iceberg_depth): Plant 2-3 threads that hint at the waiting-room/reincarnation twist in Ch 19 without spoiling it. The current foreshadowing ledger has 20 threads but none point to the novel's most important plot turn.

INTEGRATE EXTERNAL THREAT WITH MAGIC SYSTEM (affects lore_interconnection, outline_completeness): Make Meridian's detection of the island a direct consequence of Lịch's notebook-writing (more remembering = more magical signature = more visible to instruments). This transforms a modular external threat into an internally-generated dilemma and gives the outline a resolution path for the external subplot.
